feed.title: SAP Community - BW Business Explorer (SAP BEx)
feed.subtitle: BW Business Explorer (SAP BEx) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issue with tab strips
entry.content:
I’m working on a tabstrip screen that contains two tabs: Expense Logic and Attendance Logic. I’ve noticed an issue where, after clicking the second tab and executing the logic, the screen automatically reverts back to the first tab (Expense Logic) when I navigate back.

What I need is:

The selected tab should remain active after execution — it should not switch back automatically.

Could you please help me resolve this issue?

 

I’ve attached the relevant portion of my code from the program ZABAB, along with a screenshot for your reference.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF TABBED BLOCK tab_strip FOR 30 LINES.
  SELECTION-SCREEN TAB (20) tab1 USER-COMMAND tab11 DEFAULT SCREEN 0100.
  SELECTION-SCREEN TAB (40) tab2 USER-COMMAND tab22 DEFAULT SCREEN 0200.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK tab_strip.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 0100 AS SUBSCREEN.

  SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.

    PARAMETERS: r_emp  RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad1 USER-COMMAND asd1 DEFAULT 'X',
                r_dept RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad1,
                r_type RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad1.


    PARAMETERS: P_name   TYPE zemploys-emname MODIF ID m1,
                p_year   TYPE zexpense-year_no MODIF ID m2,
                p_amt    TYPE zexpense-amount MODIF ID m3,
                p_dept   TYPE zemploys-departmnt MODIF ID m4,
                p_month  TYPE zemploys-month_no MODIF ID m5,
                P_expyr  TYPE zexpense-expense_type MODIF ID m7,
                p_mntnme TYPE zexpense-mnth_nme MODIF ID m8.
    SELECT-OPTIONS: s_date FOR zexpense-expense_date MODIF ID m6.

  SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 0100.



SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 0200 AS SUBSCREEN.

  SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b2 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-002.


    PARAMETERS:
      p_att1 RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad2 USER-COMMAND tad2, "DEFAULT 'X',
      p_att2 RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad2,
      p_att3 RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad2,
      p_att4 RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad2,
      p_att5 RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad2.

    PARAMETERS: P_yrs    TYPE zattendancedet-year_no MODIF ID a2,
                P_emid   TYPE ZATTENDANCEDEt-emid MODIF ID a3 MATCHCODE OBJECT zsh_active_emp,
                P_depts  TYPE zemploys-departmnt MODIF ID a5,
                p_months TYPE ZATTENDANCEDEt-month_no MODIF ID a4.

    SELECT-OPTIONS: S_dte FOR ZATTENDANCEDEt-date_NO MODIF ID a6.

  SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b2.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 0200.


INITIALIZATION.
  tab1 = 'Expense logics'.
  tab2 = 'Attendance logics'.
  gv_active_tab = 'TAB11'.


AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  CASE sy-ucomm.
    WHEN 'TAB11' OR 'TAB22'.
      gv_active_tab = sy-ucomm.
  ENDCASE.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

  CASE gv_active_tab.
    WHEN 'TAB11'.
      tab_strip-activetab = 'TAB11'.
    WHEN 'TAB22'.
      tab_strip-activetab = 'TAB22'.
  ENDCASE.


  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF r_emp = 'X'.
      IF screen-group1 = 'M1'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M2'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M3'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M4'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M5'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M6'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M7'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M8'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    IF r_dept = 'X'.

      IF screen-group1 = 'M1'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M2'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M3'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M4'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M5'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M6'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M7'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M8'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.


    IF r_type = 'X'.

      IF screen-group1 = 'M1'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M2'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M3'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M4'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M5'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M6'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M7'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'M8'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.



  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF  p_att1 = 'X'.
      IF screen-group1 = 'A2'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A3'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A4'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A5'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A6'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    IF  p_att2 = 'X'.
      IF screen-group1 = 'A2'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A3'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A4'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A5'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A6'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    IF  p_att3 = 'X'.
      IF screen-group1 = 'A2'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A3'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A4'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A5'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A6'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    IF p_att4 = 'X'.
      IF screen-group1 = 'A2'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A3'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A4'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A5'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A6'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.


    IF p_att5 = 'X'.
      IF screen-group1 = 'A2'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A3'.
        screen-active = '1'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A4'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A5'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A6'.
        screen-active = '0'.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A7'.
      screen-active = '0'.
      MODIFY SCREEN.

    ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'A8'.
      screen-active = '0'.
      MODIFY SCREEN.
    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.


START-OF-SELECTION.

  CASE gv_active_tab.
    WHEN 'TAB11'.


      IF r_emp = 'X'.

        SELECT a~emid,
               a~emname,
               a~departmnt,
               a~status,
               a~month_no,
               a~year_no,
               b~expense_date,
               b~expense_type,
               b~amount
          FROM zemploys AS a
          INNER JOIN zexpense AS b
            ON a~emid = b~emid
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @IT_table.

        LOOP AT it_table INTO wa_table.

          IF p_month IS INITIAL AND p_year IS INITIAL AND p_dept IS INITIAL
                      AND p_amt IS INITIAL AND s_date IS INITIAL.

            lv_total_expense = lv_total_expense + wa_table-amount.

            WRITE: /  'Grand Total Expense:', lv_total_expense,
                      'Name        :', wa_table-emname,
                      'Department  :', wa_table-departmnt,
                      'Month       :', wa_table-month_no,
                      'Year        :', wa_table-year_no,
                      'Type        :', wa_table-expense_type,
                      'Amount      :', wa_table-amount.


          ELSEIF p_month IS NOT INITIAL AND p_year IS NOT INITIAL
             AND wa_table-month_no = p_month AND wa_table-year_no = p_year.

            WRITE: / 'Name        :', wa_table-emname,
                     'Month       :', wa_table-month_no,
                     'Year        :', wa_table-year_no,
                     'Amount      :', wa_table-amount.


          ELSEIF  p_dept IS NOT INITIAL AND p_month IS INITIAL AND p_year IS INITIAL
                 AND wa_table-departmnt = p_dept.

            lv_total_expense = lv_total_expense + wa_table-amount.


            WRITE: / 'Name        :', wa_table-emname,
                     'Month       :', wa_table-month_no,
                     'Year        :', wa_table-year_no,
                     'Department  :' , wa_table-departmnt,
                     'Amount      :', wa_table-amount.


          ELSEIF p_amt IS NOT INITIAL AND s_date IS NOT INITIAL
                     AND wa_table-expense_date IN s_date AND wa_table-amount >= p_amt.

            lv_total_expense = lv_total_expense + wa_table-amount.

            WRITE: / 'Date   :', wa_table-expense_date,
                     'Amount :', wa_table-amount.


          ELSEIF p_year IS NOT INITIAL AND p_month IS INITIAL AND p_dept IS INITIAL
                AND s_date IS INITIAL AND p_amt IS INITIAL
                AND wa_table-year_no = p_year.

            lv_total_expense = lv_total_expense + wa_table-amount.

            WRITE: / 'Grand Total Expense:', lv_total_expense,
                   'Month       :', wa_table-month_no,
                   'Year        :', wa_table-year_no.


          ENDIF.


        ENDLOOP.

      ELSEIF r_dept = 'X'.


        IF p_month IS INITIAL AND p_year IS INITIAL.

          SELECT emid,
                 month_no,
                 year_no,
                 SUM( amount ) AS total_exp
            INTO TABLE @DATA(it_total_expense)
            FROM zexpense
            GROUP BY emid, month_no, year_no.

        ELSE.

          SELECT emid,
                 month_no,
                 year_no,
                 SUM( amount ) AS total_exp
            FROM zexpense
            WHERE ( month_no IS NOT INITIAL AND month_no = @p_month )
              AND ( year_no  IS NOT INITIAL AND year_no  = @p_year  )
            GROUP BY emid, month_no, year_no
             INTO TABLE @IT_total_expense.

        ENDIF.

        LOOP AT it_total_expense INTO DATA(wa_total_expense).

          " Check if entry exists in ZMONTHSALARIES


          SELECT SINGLE emid
            FROM zmonthsalaries
            WHERE emid     = @wa_total_expense-emid
              AND month_no = @wa_total_expense-month_no
              AND year_no  = @wa_total_expense-year_no
            INTO @DATA(lv_exist_emid).

          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            zmonthsalaries-emid              = wa_total_expense-emid.
            zmonthsalaries-month_no          = wa_total_expense-month_no.
            zmonthsalaries-year_no           = wa_total_expense-year_no.
            zmonthsalaries-totalexpenses     = wa_total_expense-total_exp.
            zmonthsalaries-last_updated_date = sy-datum.
            zmonthsalaries-last_updated_time = sy-uzeit.

            MODIFY zmonthsalaries FROM zmonthsalaries.
            WRITE: / 'Updated:', wa_total_expense-emid, 'Total Expense:', wa_total_expense-total_exp.

          ELSE.
            "                Insert
            zmonthsalaries-emid              = wa_total_expense-emid.
            zmonthsalaries-month_no          = wa_total_expense-month_no.
            zmonthsalaries-year_no           = wa_total_expense-year_no.
            zmonthsalaries-totalexpenses     = wa_total_expense-total_exp.
            zmonthsalaries-last_updated_date = sy-datum.
            zmonthsalaries-last_updated_time = sy-uzeit.

            INSERT zmonthsalaries FROM zmonthsalaries.
            WRITE: / 'Inserted:', wa_total_expense-emid, 'Total Expense:', wa_total_expense-total_exp.

          ENDIF.

        ENDLOOP.


        ELSEIF  R_TYPE = 'X'.

          CLEAR: ITK_TABLE, WAK_TABLE.

          SELECT A~EMID,
                 A~EMNAME,
                 A~DEPARTMNT,
                 B~MONTH_NO,
                 B~MNTH_NME,
                 B~YEAR_NO,
                 SUM( B~AMOUNT ) AS TOTALEXPENSES
            INTO TABLE @ITK_TABLE
            FROM ZEMPLOYS AS A
            INNER JOIN ZEXPENSE AS B ON A~EMID = B~EMID
            GROUP BY A~EMID, A~EMNAME, A~DEPARTMNT, B~MONTH_NO, B~MNTH_NME, B~YEAR_NO.


            LOOP AT ITK_TABLE INTO WAK_TABLE.


              WRITE: / 'Employee:', WAK_TABLE-EMID, WAK_TABLE-EMNAME,
                       'Dept:', WAK_TABLE-DEPARTMNT,
                       'Month:', WAK_TABLE-MNTH_NME,
                       'Year:', WAK_TABLE-YEAR_NO,
                       'Total Expense:', WAK_TABLE-TOTALEXPENSES.

            ENDLOOP.

          ENDIF.


    WHEN 'TAB22'.

      IF  p_att1 = 'X'.


        IF p_months IS INITIAL AND p_yrs IS INITIAL.



          SELECT emid,
                 month_no,
                 year_no,
            COUNT(*) AS present_days
            INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @IT_attendance
            FROM zattendancedet
            GROUP BY emid, month_no, year_no.


        ELSEIF p_months IS NOT INITIAL AND p_yrs IS NOT INITIAL .


          SELECT emid,
                 month_no,
                 year_no,
                 COUNT(*) AS present_days
               INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @IT_attendance
               FROM zattendancedet
               WHERE month_no = @p_months
               AND year_no = @P_yrs
               AND atendnce_stus = 'P'
               GROUP BY emid, month_no, year_no.
        ENDIF.

        LOOP AT it_attendance INTO wa_ATTENDANCE.

          WRITE: / 'Employee ID:',wa_ATTENDANCE-emid,
                   'Year:', wa_ATTENDANCE-year_no,
                   'Month:',wa_ATTENDANCE-month_no,
                   'Total Present Days:',wa_ATTENDANCE-present_days.

        ENDLOOP.




      ELSEIF  p_att2 = 'X'.


        IF p_months IS INITIAL AND p_yrs IS INITIAL AND S_dte IS INITIAL AND p_depts IS INITIAL.

          SELECT zemploys~emid,
                     zemploys~departmnt,
                     zattendancedet~month_no,
                     zattendancedet~year_no,
        zattendancedet~date_no,
                     zemploys~emname,


                 SUM( CASE WHEN zattendancedet~atendnce_stus = 'P' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END ) AS present_days,
                 SUM( CASE WHEN zattendancedet~atendnce_stus = 'A' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END ) AS absent_days,
                   SUM( CASE WHEN zattendancedet~atendnce_stus = 'L' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END ) AS leave_days
                INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @IT_attendance
                FROM Zemploys
               INNER JOIN zattendancedet
            ON zemploys~emid = zattendancedet~emid
                GROUP BY zemploys~emid,
                         zemploys~departmnt,
                         zattendancedet~month_no,
                           Zemploys~emname,
                         zattendancedet~year_no,
                         zattendancedet~date_NO.


        ELSEIF  p_months IS NOT INITIAL AND p_yrs IS NOT INITIAL AND S_dte IS  NOT INITIAL AND p_depts IS NOT INITIAL .


          SELECT zemploys~emid,
                     zemploys~departmnt,
                     zattendancedet~month_no,
                     zattendancedet~year_no,
                   zattendancedet~date_no,
                     zemploys~emname  ,

                 SUM( CASE WHEN zattendancedet~atendnce_stus = 'P' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END ) AS present_days,
                 SUM( CASE WHEN zattendancedet~atendnce_stus = 'A' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END ) AS absent_days,
                   SUM( CASE WHEN zattendancedet~atendnce_stus = 'L' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END ) AS leave_days
                INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @IT_attendance
                FROM Zemploys
                INNER JOIN zattendancedet
            ON zemploys~emid = zattendancedet~emid
            WHERE zemploys~departmnt = @p_depts
              AND zattendancedet~month_no = @p_months
              AND zattendancedet~year_no = @p_yrs
              AND zattendancedet~Date_no IN @S_dte
                GROUP BY zemploys~emid,
                         zemploys~departmnt,
                         zattendancedet~month_no,
                           Zemploys~emname,
                         zattendancedet~year_no,
                   zattendancedet~date_NO.

        ENDIF.
        LOOP AT it_attendance INTO wa_attendance.
          WRITE: / 'Department:', wa_attendance-departmnt,
                   'Employee ID:', wa_attendance-emid,
                   'Employee Name:', wa_attendance-emname,
                   'Year:', wa_attendance-year_no,
                   'Month:', wa_attendance-month_no,
               'Date:', wa_attendance-date_no,

     'Total Present Days:',wa_attendance-present_days,
                   'Total Absent Days:', wa_attendance-absent_days,
                 'Total Leave Days:', wa_attendance-leave_days.

        ENDLOOP.



      ELSEIF p_att3 = 'X'.

        IF p_months IS INITIAL AND p_yrs IS INITIAL AND p_emid IS INITIAL.

          SELECT emid,
                 month_no,
                 year_no,
                 COUNT(*) AS present_days
            INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @LT_present_data
            FROM zattendancedet
            GROUP BY emid, month_no, year_no.


        ELSEIF  p_months IS NOT INITIAL AND p_yrs IS NOT INITIAL AND p_emid IS  NOT INITIAL .

          SELECT emid,
                 month_no,
                 year_no,
                 COUNT(*) AS present_days
            INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @LT_present_data
            FROM zattendancedet
            WHERE atendnce_stus = 'P'
              AND month_no = @p_months
              AND year_no  = @p_yrs
            AND emid     = @p_emid
            GROUP BY emid, month_no, year_no.
        ENDIF.
        LOOP AT lt_present_data INTO ls_present_data.

          CLEAR wa_attendance_summary.
          wa_attendance_summary-emid         = ls_present_data-emid.
          wa_attendance_summary-month_no     = ls_present_data-month_no.
          wa_attendance_summary-year_no      = ls_present_data-year_no.
          wa_attendance_summary-present_days = ls_present_data-present_days.

          SELECT COUNT(*) INTO @wa_attendance_summary-workingdays
            FROM zattendancedet
            WHERE emid     = @LS_present_data-emid
              AND month_no = @LS_present_data-month_no
              AND year_no  = @LS_present_data-year_no.

          IF wa_attendance_summary-workingdays > 0.
            wa_attendance_summary-attendnpercent =
              ( ls_present_data-present_days * 100 ) / wa_attendance_summary-workingdays.
          ELSE.
            wa_attendance_summary-attendnpercent = 0.
          ENDIF.

          APPEND wa_attendance_summary TO it_attendance_summary.

        ENDLOOP.

        WRITE: / 'EMID', 'MONTH', 'YEAR','WORKINGDAYS','PRESENTDAYS', 'ATTENDANCE %'.


        LOOP AT it_attendance_summary INTO wa_attendance_summary.
          WRITE: / wa_attendance_summary-emid,
                   wa_attendance_summary-month_no,
                   wa_attendance_summary-year_no,
                   wa_attendance_summary-workingdays,
                   wa_attendance_summary-present_days,
                   wa_attendance_summary-attendnpercent.
        ENDLOOP.

*      endif.



      ELSEIF p_att4 = 'X'.

  IF p_months IS INITIAL AND p_yrs IS INITIAL AND p_emid IS INITIAL.

    SELECT emid,
             date_no,
             day_no,
             atendnce_stus,
             leave_typ,
             year_no,
             month_no
        INTO TABLE @LT_att
        FROM zattendancedet.

  elseif p_months IS not INITIAL AND p_yrs IS not INITIAL AND p_emid IS not INITIAL.


             SELECT emid,
             date_no,
             day_no,
             atendnce_stus,
             leave_typ,
             year_no,
             month_no
        INTO TABLE @LT_att
        FROM zattendancedet
        WHERE year_no  = @p_yrs
     AND emid     = @p_emid
     AND month_no = @p_months.


ENDif.



        WRITE:/ sy-uline(60).

        WRITE: / 'Employee Attendance Details - Month:', p_months, 'Year:', p_yrs, 60 sy-vline.

        WRITE:/ sy-uline(60).


        WRITE:/ sy-uline(130).

        WRITE:/ 'EmpID' COLOR 2,20 sy-vline,'Date' COLOR 2,40 sy-vline,'Day' COLOR 2,60 sy-vline,'atendnce stus' COLOR 2, 80 sy-vline, 'Leave Type' COLOR 2, 100 sy-vline, 'year' COLOR 2, 120 sy-vline,'Month',130 sy-vline.

        WRITE:/ sy-uline(130).


        "Loop and Display Records
        LOOP AT lt_att INTO wa_att.

          WRITE:/ wa_att-emid COLOR 2,20 sy-vline, wa_att-date_no DD/MM/YYYY COLOR 2,40 sy-vline,wa_att-day_no COLOR 2,60 sy-vline, wa_att-atendnce_stus COLOR 2, 80 sy-vline,  wa_att-leave_typ COLOR 2, 100 sy-vline,wa_att-month_no COLOR 2, 120 sy-vline,
          wa_att-year_no, 130 sy-vline.


          " Count attendance categories
          CASE wa_att-atendnce_stus.
            WHEN 'P'.
              lv_present = lv_present + 1.
            WHEN 'A'.
              lv_absent  = lv_absent + 1.
            WHEN 'L'.
              lv_leave   = lv_leave + 1.
          ENDCASE.

        ENDLOOP.

        "Summary Section
        lv_total = lv_present + lv_absent + lv_leave.

        WRITE:/ sy-uline(130).

        WRITE: / 'Summary:'.
        WRITE: / 'Total Working Days:', lv_total.
        WRITE: / 'Total Present     :', lv_present.
        WRITE: / 'Total Absent      :', lv_absent.
        WRITE: / 'Total Leave       :', lv_leave.




      ELSEIF p_att5 = 'X'.

IF p_emid IS INITIAL.

          SELECT emid,
                 emname
               INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @IT_attendance
               FROM zemploys.


        ELSEIF p_emid IS not INITIAL.

          SELECT emid,
                 emname
               INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @IT_attendance
               FROM zemploys
             WHERE emid = @p_emid.

        ENDIF.

        LOOP AT it_attendance INTO wa_ATTENDANCE.

          WRITE: / 'Employee ID:',wa_ATTENDANCE-emid,
                   'Name:', wa_ATTENDANCE-emname.
        ENDLOOP.

   ENDIF.
  ENDCASE.

entry.title: Bex filter info Object search by text fails
entry.content:
Hi

I have a basic sample query with one ifoobject 0material__0prodh3

I"ve set it to retrieve data from mater data table

When I run the query and try to filter that infoobjet, I manage to find my object when I search by key but not by text

I even tried to look in the basic text table /BI0/TPROD_HIER
And I get similar behavior

 

Any ideas?

 

Thanks

Shlomi

entry.title: Possible to create multiple sales order in one go through any T.code?
entry.content:
Hi,

In our organization sometime we have to create more than 600 sales order in one go. Is there any T-code so we can create it bulk?

entry.title: We wanted to know the Exact estimation which we needed for Bulk Sales Process Automation
entry.content:
Dear All,

Can we get the detailed estimation for below things in technical and functional days format?

Requirement to handle the following processes in bulk:
• Create Sales Orders in bulk
• Convert Sales Orders to Tax Invoices in bulk
• Generate Proforma Invoices in bulk
• Generate bulk outputs for both Proforma and Tax Invoices

entry.title: Transport of analytical query variants
entry.content:
Hello,

Is it possible to transport analytical query variant created with queries from the Query Browser app (F1068)? I cannot find any documentation on this subject. Look forward to your response. 

 

Kind regards,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP Precalculation Server "ERROR: program BIPRECXY not registered"
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

i am currently installing SAP precalculation server for SAP BW. When creating an server via transaction "rsprecalcadmin" i am not able to register the TP.

In SM59 the connection check of the created RFC connection shows following error:

I did set the gw/acl_mode to 0.

Reginfo is configured to allow any registrations.

 

Greetings
Rouven

entry.title: SAP bw bex report I have issue with japan currency divding by 100 kigfigure for current period
entry.content:
Hi Team 

Please help me I have issue with only JAPAN Currency inthe SAP BEX report value showing divided by 100, but remaining countries currency are working fine , This issue for only current period for Japan. But when I check infoprovoder  it's correct values for Japan .

Could you please provide me the solution.

 

Thank you 

Anil Kumar Busarapu

entry.title: Marketplace App Deployment & Lifecycle Management Using SAP DevOps Tools
entry.content:
In this discussion, we aim to explore the complete process of deploying and managing the lifecycle of a marketplace app development project using SAP DevOps tools. The goal is to understand how developers, DevOps engineers, and project managers can streamline continuous integration, testing, and delivery within the SAP ecosystem.

We’ll discuss best practices for:

Automating deployment pipelines for marketplace applications.

Integrating SAP Cloud Platform, CI/CD, and version control systems.

Managing updates, bug fixes, and scalability throughout the app’s lifecycle.

Ensuring security, compliance, and performance optimization in live environments.

Real-world challenges faced during app deployment and how SAP DevOps tools can help overcome them.

What to discuss:

What are your preferred SAP DevOps tools or workflows for marketplace app deployment?

How do you handle version control, rollbacks, and testing in a live environment?

Any insights or lessons learned from your own deployment experiences?

Let’s collaborate and share strategies, tools, and practical insights to make marketplace app deployment smoother, faster, and more reliable.

entry.title: Configuration for Setting Up Reminders for DP Exceptions in SAP VIM
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Is there any configuration available in SAP VIM to set up reminders for DP (Document Processing) Exceptions? Could you please explain the configuration steps or process involved in enabling and managing these reminders?

@vamsixk .

entry.title: SAP BW filtering with contains pattern
entry.content:
Dear collegues !
In SAP BW4/hana in a customer exit I would want to take the value from a variable take everything befure '-' and filter in another variable with * sign around it with the contains pattern. 
In another world if my variable hold 23141424-001-A  I would like to take the part *23141424* and filter with contains pattern in the another variable.

 

  Data: W_A_VAR_RANGE like LINE OF  I_T_VAR_RANGE,
      L_S_RANGE like LINE OF C_T_RANGE,
      searchText like W_A_VAR_RANGE-LOW,
      lv_result TYPE string .

CASE I_STEP.

when 1.
when 2.

   if  I_VNAM  =  'AQV_AMATERIAL_EOON'.
     Loop at I_T_VAR_RANGE into W_A_VAR_RANGE  where  VNAM = 'AQV_AMATERIAL_EOOI' and SIGN = 'I' and OPT = 'EQ' .
       SEARCHText = W_A_VAR_RANGE-LOW.
        SHIFT  SEARCHText LEFT DELETING LEADING '0'.

          SPLIT  SEARCHText AT '-' INTO lv_result DATA(lv_rest).
          clear searchtext.
          CONCATENATE '*' lv_result '*' into searchtext.
      W_A_VAR_RANGE-VNAM = 'AQV_AMATERIAL_EOON'.
       W_A_VAR_RANGE-OPT = 'CP'.
       W_A_VAR_RANGE-LOW = searchtext.
        MOVE-CORRESPONDING  W_A_VAR_RANGE to L_S_RANGE.
        append L_S_RANGE to c_t_range.

     endloop.

   endif.
endcase.
  endmethod.

 

The logic works fine but when I run the query it runs on an error.

As soon as I change the CP in the code to EQ the problem disappear. 
Can it be that in customer exits the contains pattern cannot be used  for filtering the query ?
If you could use it could you please give me an example code that it works with please ? 
I am using customer exit variable with multiple single values, because I will have several values.

Any help taken kindly thank you

entry.title: 0EMPLOYEE_ATTR & PA0000(CDS View) BW4HANA
entry.content:
hello All! 

Could anyone assist me? I’m creating a CompositeProvider in Eclipse for BW/4HANA modeling. I need to filter records for Transfer Postings, so I applied the MASSN code Z7 in PA0000 to extract all transfer-posting cases. To retrieve the latest updated information, I’m joining these PERNRs with PA0001, applying the filter ENDDA = 31.12.9999, so I can get the corresponding PERSK and PERSG values for the most current records.

Since the PA0001 data is available through the standard InfoObject 0EMPLOYEE_ATTR, I use that to retrieve PERSG and PERSK. However, for PA0000, I created a custom DataSource with code to extract the required MASSN-based records.

 

define view ZCDS_PA0000_Z7 as select from pa0000

 

 

{

key concat( pernr, begda )as ZCONC_KEY_UNIQUE,

 

key pernr,

key begda,

// key endda,

massn

}

 

where

 

//massn = 'Z4'

//or

massn = 'Z7'

 

Here is the rephrased version in a clear and professional way:

I mapped the start date (BEGDA) field to the standard InfoObject 0VALIDFROM.
When I run the InfoObject independently, it works fine and returns accurate results.
However, when I combine it with 0EMPLOYEE_ATTR, the output becomes incorrect for the condition ENDDA = 31.12.9999.

Whether I use a left outer join or an inner join, the issue remains the same:

  • PA0000 gives 199 records (MASSN = Z7)

  • PA0001 / 0EMPLOYEE_ATTR gives 153 updated records

  • But in the CompositeProvider, it still shows 199 records instead of 153, even though 0EMPLOYEE_ATTR should return only the updated records.

I am not sure why this mismatch is happening, since 0EMPLOYEE_ATTR is supposed to provide only the latest active record with ENDDA = 31.12.9999.

entry.title: How to create a maintenance plan, next planned date shall be considered based on the previous mainte
entry.content:
How to create a maintenance plan, next planned date shall be considered based on the previous maintenance call completion date.

What is the strategy parameters for this case

entry.title: BEx query with totals and plan by user input periods
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have requirement where user will input Fiscal period for example Jul.2025-Jun.2025 than query should display in rows item and branches and columns Unit, Price, estimated and budget values should display total for user input period whereas plan should display by period. Please find below screenshot for more details. 

My question is how to create structure where some keyfigures should show totals while other plan keyfigures should display plan data by month. Please suggest,  all your help and advise are welcome and greatly appreciated and points rewarded.

entry.title: Consolidation Plan & Actual Data
entry.content:
I want to consolidate Actual and Plan data, but I am facing an issue. The Actual data is coming from /FIGL14, which contains Cost Center, GL Account, and Amount. On the other hand, the planning Model X contains Commitment Item, GL Account, and Fund Center.

The challenge is that /FIGL14 does not contain Commitment Item and Fund Center, so I am unsure how to consolidate the data between these two structures. Could you please advise on how this can be handled?

 

If I use a left outer join, these are the values I am getting. However, the issue is that in the Actuals, the value is appearing against the GL Account instead of the Commitment Item. As a result, the actual data is showing only for the GL account, not for the commitment item.

entry.title: Report converted amount is not matching with manual conversion
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

In BW4HANA as per requirement created new key figure and added to report, to that key figure variable is assigned created in RSCUR which has exchange rate type is P and convert local currency to target currency EUR.

So converted amount us coming but user is checking manually with dividing exchange rate with target amount values are getting with some minor difference what could be the reason.

in excel column D is not matching with manual calculation column E

entry.title: Hi I would like to know if Dunning OPD is supported in Private Cloud?
entry.content: Hi I would like to know if Dunning OPD is supported in Private Cloud?

entry.title: Sap tds enhancement
entry.content:
Client has multiple company codes:
Company code A = 1000
Company code B = 2000
Company code C = 3000
Same vendor supplies to all company codes.
As per Indian Income Tax Section 194Q, TDS should apply once cumulative purchases exceed ₹50,00,000 across all mapped company codes.
Standard SAP checks threshold per company code only, 

How i can do enhancement .

entry.title: How Growing Businesses Simplify Complex Operations with an Integrated ERP System
entry.content:
In the world of commerce, business growth often brings increased operational complexity. What once worked well for a small team—spreadsheets, point solutions, and manual processes—can quickly become inefficient and difficult to manage as the company expands.

For many growing businesses, this shift is gradual. What starts as a flexible setup soon turns into daily firefighting—reconciling data, tracking inventory manually, and waiting on reports from multiple teams.

As organizations grow, many leaders begin looking for better ways to manage operations, improve visibility, and maintain efficiency. This is where an integrated Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system can make a significant difference.

By connecting key business functions into one platform, an ERP system helps growing businesses simplify operations and gain better control over their processes.

The Business Problem: Disconnected Systems and Data Silos

In their early growth stages, companies often use different tools to manage various business functions. One software might handle accounting, another inventory, while a CRM manages sales activities—and operational data may still be stored in spreadsheets. Although this approach seems manageable at first, it gradually creates operational challenges that impact clarity and efficiency:

  • Disconnected systems – Departments work on different platforms that do not communicate with each other
  • Data stored in silos – Important information is spread across multiple systems and files
  • Frequent data errors – Manual data entry increases the likelihood of mistakes and inconsistencies
  • Overdependence on spreadsheets – Teams rely heavily on Excel for reporting, tracking, and reconciliation

Over time, what once felt manageable becomes difficult to control, often leading to confusion, delays, and limited visibility for decision-making.

The Consequences of Fragmented Operations

When business systems are not integrated, everyday operations can quickly become time-consuming and error-prone. While the impact may not be obvious initially, inefficiencies grow as the business scales.

Some common challenges include:

  • 1. Data duplication
    The same data is entered into multiple systems, consuming valuable time and often leading to costly discrepancies.
  • 2. Inventory mismatches
    Without centralized visibility, stock records may not match actual inventory—leading to stockouts, overstocking, or delayed deliveries.
  • 3. Slow decision-making
    Generating reports requires manual consolidation, delaying access to accurate insights when they are needed most.
  • 4. Process inefficiency
    Manual workflows create bottlenecks in approvals, reporting, and day-to-day operations.

These issues not only affect internal productivity but also impact customer experience and overall business performance.

Why These Problems Occur as Businesses Grow

As businesses expand, operational complexity increases naturally. A growing company must manage:

  • Multiple departments
  • Large customer and vendor databases
  • Expanding product catalogues
  • More complex supply chains
  • Higher transaction volumes

Without integrated systems, managing these moving parts through disconnected tools becomes increasingly difficult. Teams end up spending more time reconciling information than using it to make strategic decisions.

Eventually, many businesses reach a stage where they need a unified system that provides a single, reliable source of truth across the organization.

Signs Your Business May Need an ERP System

Many growing businesses notice operational strain as they scale. If your team frequently encounters the following situations, it may indicate that your current systems are limiting your growth:

  • Heavy reliance on spreadsheets and manual reporting
  • Difficulty tracking inventory accurately
  • Multiple software systems that do not communicate with each other
  • Lack of real-time visibility into financial or operational data
  • Increasing time spent reconciling data across departments

When these challenges start affecting efficiency and decision-making, it is often a strong signal that an integrated ERP system may be needed.

Before ERP vs After ERP

The difference between operating with disconnected systems and an integrated ERP can be significant:

Without ERPWith Integrated ERP
Multiple disconnected systemsSingle unified platform
Manual data entryAutomated data flow
Delayed reportingReal-time insights
Inventory discrepanciesAccurate inventory visibility
Limited collaborationCross-departmental visibility

How an Integrated ERP System Solves These Challenges

An integrated ERP system connects key business functions into a single platform, enabling organizations to operate more efficiently and with greater clarity.

Instead of relying on multiple disconnected tools, businesses can access real-time operational and financial data from one centralized system.

Key benefits include:

1. Centralized data management

All business information—from finance and sales to inventory and procurement—is stored in one system, eliminating data silos and ensuring consistency.

2. Improved accuracy

Automated data flows reduce manual entry, minimizing errors and rework.

3. Real-time visibility

Decision-makers gain instant access to accurate reports and dashboards, enabling faster and more confident decisions.

4. Streamlined processes

Automated workflows reduce bottlenecks and improve overall efficiency.

5. Better collaboration

Teams across departments can work with shared, up-to-date information, improving coordination and execution.

For many growing organizations, ERP becomes the foundation for scaling operations while maintaining control and visibility.

How SAP Business One Addresses These Challenges Across Industries

While the need for integration is common across businesses, the nature of operational challenges varies by industry. Solutions like SAP Business One are designed specifically for small and mid-sized businesses, helping them streamline processes while remaining flexible and scalable.

Here’s how this translates across different sectors:

Manufacturing: Improving Production Planning and Visibility

As manufacturing businesses grow, managing production becomes more complex. Common challenges include:

  • Difficulty aligning production schedules with available inventory
  • Limited visibility into raw material requirements
  • Manual tracking of work orders
  • Poor coordination between procurement, production, and sales

An integrated ERP system brings structure to these processes. With SAP Business One, manufacturers can manage bill of materials (BOMs), material requirements planning (MRP), and production schedules within a single system—ensuring materials are available when needed and production stays aligned with demand.

Distribution and Trading: Achieving Real-Time Inventory Control

For distributors and trading companies, inventory management becomes increasingly challenging across multiple locations.

Common issues include:

  • Mismatches between system records and actual stock
  • Difficulty tracking stock movement
  • Limited visibility into fast- and slow-moving items
  • Inefficient order fulfilment

With SAP Business One, businesses gain real-time visibility into inventory across warehouses, enabling better demand planning and more reliable order commitments.

FMCG and e-Commerce: Managing High-Volume, Multi-Channel Operations Efficiently

FMCG and e-Commerce businesses often face increased complexity as they scale across multiple sales channels, warehouses, and customer touchpoints.

Challenges include:

  • Inconsistent inventory data across warehouses and online platforms
  • Difficulty consolidating sales, returns, and financial data across channels
  • Stock-outs or overstocking of fast-moving products
  • Limited visibility into demand patterns and customer buying behaviour

An integrated ERP system centralizes data across all channels, enabling businesses to manage inventory more effectively, streamline order fulfilment, manage order returns, and make faster, data-driven decisions around demand planning, pricing, and promotions.

Service-Oriented Businesses: Improving Project and Financial Management

Service organizations such as consulting or IT firms often struggle with:

  • Tracking project costs and profitability
  • Coordinating teams and resources
  • Delays in invoicing
  • Limited visibility into project progress

With SAP Business One, businesses can integrate project tracking, financials, and resource planning—providing a clear view of project performance and improving overall control.

What to Consider Before Choosing an ERP System

Selecting the right ERP system is a critical decision that can directly impact how effectively your business scales. Beyond features, it’s important to evaluate how well the solution aligns with your current needs and future growth plans.

Key considerations include:

  • Scalability Can the system support your business as it grows in terms of users, transactions, and operational complexity?
  • Industry relevance Does the ERP offer functionalities that align with your industry processes, such as manufacturing, distribution, retail, or services?
  • Ease of use and adoption Is the system intuitive enough for your teams to use effectively without extensive dependency on manual workarounds?
  • Real-time visibility Does it provide accurate, real-time insights across finance, inventory, and operations?
  • Integration capabilities Can it connect seamlessly with your existing tools and future systems?
  • Implementation and support Do you have access to the right expertise to ensure smooth implementation and ongoing optimization?

Solutions like SAP Business One ERP are designed with these considerations in mind. Built specifically for small and mid-sized businesses, it offers a balance of functionality, flexibility, and scalability—while integrating core business processes into a single, easy-to-use platform.

Bringing It All Together

As businesses grow, managing operations through disconnected systems becomes increasingly challenging. An integrated ERP system addresses this by centralizing data, improving visibility, and streamlining processes across departments.

The result is a more structured, efficient, and scalable way to manage operations—without the need for constant manual intervention.

Is Your Business Ready for ERP?

If your current systems are creating inefficiencies or limiting visibility, it may be time to evaluate whether your business is ready for ERP.

At RPM Infotech, we work with growing businesses to assess their existing processes, identify gaps, and recommend the right ERP approach based on their specific needs. We combine domain expertise with a practical understanding of business operations to ensure smooth implementation, faster adoption, and long-term success.

Start with an ERP evaluation for your business—and take the first step toward more streamlined and scalable operations.

entry.title: Table in BW Web template is shrinking if data is blank
entry.content: We have a 4 Week report (Web Template) where we are having 2 tables in the report, the first table will have the data for all categories by week wise and the other table will have the data at day level for the last week. If there is no data in the 4 week table, the column in the day wise table will shrink and the formatting is wrong., due to this the data is not showing correctly in the report and misleading the data in the report

entry.title: Bex selection filters are not working as expected once transported
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an issue as below in selection parameters

1. when I add the WBS to the selection filters of 0WBS_ELEMT in rows selection (directly in quality system I can see the data)

2, but when I do the same in development collect in RT and moved to QA, it is not working.

Prior check

1. I did analyze the master data existence, it is available in both systems (Development and Quality)

2, Tried transporting by recollecting the selections in QA number of time but no luck

3. Done RSRT generate query in both development and Quality system not working

4. Checked the RSDIRCOMP by selecting the affected query I can see three version (A, B M)

Environment

BW4HANA

Please help if you have any suggestions

 

Thanks & Regards
